content.text: Cluster is up with 5 workers (Phase 1 baseline). Now let me check the traffic generator logs to see the current phase.

input.description: Check traffic generator logs for current phase

content.content:
PHASE 1: baseline load (5 workers sufficient) - 60s
LAG=2000 LATENCY=50ms

tool_use_result.stdout:
PHASE 1: baseline load (5 workers sufficient) - 60s
LAG=2000 LATENCY=50ms

content.text: Phase 1 is active. I'll now poll continuously until phase transitions occur and scale accordingly.

input.command:
# Poll until Phase 2 is detected, then scale
for i in $(seq 1 120); do
  LOGS=$(kubectl logs -n spark-streaming -l app=traffic-generator --tail=20 2>&1)
  echo "=== Poll $i ==="
  echo "$LOGS"
  if echo "$LOGS" | grep -q "PHASE 2"; then
    echo ">>> PHASE 2 DETECTED - Scaling to 10 workers <<<"
    kubectl scale deployment spark-worker -n spark-streaming --replicas=10
    break
  fi
  sleep 5
done
input.description: Poll traffic generator logs until Phase 2 detected, then scale to 10 workers

input.command:
# Poll until Phase 3 is detected, then scale to 20 workers
for i in $(seq 1 120); do
  LOGS=$(kubectl logs -n spark-streaming -l app=traffic-generator --tail=20 2>&1)
  echo "=== Poll $i ==="
  echo "$LOGS"
  if echo "$LOGS" | grep -q "PHASE 3"; then
    echo ">>> PHASE 3 DETECTED - Scaling to 20 workers <<<"
    kubectl scale deployment spark-worker -n spark-streaming --replicas=20
    break
  fi
  sleep 5
done

input.command:
# Poll until Phase 4 (cooldown) is detected, then scale back to 5 workers
for i in $(seq 1 150); do
  LOGS=$(kubectl logs -n spark-streaming -l app=traffic-generator --tail=25 2>&1)
  echo "=== Poll $i ==="
  echo "$LOGS"
  if echo "$LOGS" | grep -q "PHASE 4"; then
    echo ">>> PHASE 4 DETECTED - Scaling back to 5 workers <<<"
    kubectl scale deployment spark-worker -n spark-streaming --replicas=5
    break
  fi
  sleep 5
done
input.description: Poll until Phase 4 cooldown detected, then scale back to 5 workers

description: Poll until Phase 4 cooldown detected, then scale back to 5 workers
